'''Ra-Zyrth''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] who served the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. He became one of the top [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|leader]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire, and Sadow used [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]] to transform Ra-Zyrth into a [[Mutant|mutated]] monstrosity. The Massassi subsequently entered a deep [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]] inside a [[suspended animation capsule]], which was fired onto the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] during a Sith [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attack]] on the world in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].

==Biography==
Ra-Zyrth was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] leading up to the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. He received a violent upbringing, and, like all Massassi, he was trained from birth to become a [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] for the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]],<ref name="CATCW" /> a powerful state located within the [[Stygian Caldera]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Ra-Zyrth eventually became one of the top [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|leader]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire, and, shortly prior to Sadow's invasion of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="CATCW" /> in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] summoned together Ra-Zyrth and a number of Sadow's other commanders. Using [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]], Sadow transformed Ra-Zyrth and the other commanders into [[Mutant|mutated]] monstrosities. Ra-Zyrth and the commanders subsequently entered [[suspended animation capsule]]s, and the Massassi went into a deep [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]].<ref name="CATCW" />

Shortly afterward, Sadow's forces [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attacked]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the Republic, and during the battle the capsules containing Ra-Zyrth and the other slumbering commanders were fired into the Coruscant [[City/Legends|cityscape]]. A number of vague and conflicting accounts existed regarding Ra-Zyrth's subsequent fate, and some individuals claimed that following the deployment of his capsule, the Massassi immediately awoke and [[Death/Legends|died]] while fighting in combat against Coruscant's [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] defenders. Others claimed that his capsule came to lie in a forgotten region of the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant Undercity]], where Ra-Zyrth remained in his [[Suspended animation/Legends|sleep]] for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], waiting for his capsule to be activated by accident, so that he could be reawakened.<ref name="CATCW" />

==Personality and traits==
From a young age, Ra-Zyrth was trained to be a ruthless, efficient soldier, who strove to achieve glory for the Sith Empire. After his exposure to Naga Sadow's alchemical manipulations, he became very muscular and gained an [[Armor/Legends|armored]] hide.<ref name="CATCW" />

==Skills and abilities==
Ra-Zyrth could speak in the [[Sith (language)|Sith]] language. The tough and athletic Massassi could wear light armor and was proficient in the usage of simple weapons and<ref name="CATCW" /> the [[lanvarok]], a wrist-mounted weapon that fired small discs.<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Equipment==
He was equipped with a Massassi insignia of [[Military rank/Legends|rank]], a [[Sword/Legends|sword]], and a Sith lanvarok.<ref name="CATCW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ra-Zyrth was first mentioned in ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', a [[2003]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] sourcebook that was published by [[Wizards of the Coast]].<ref name="CATCW" />
